sections.paragraph: (perh. “belonging to a village,” from חִוָּה =חַוָּה No. 2). A Gentile noun, a Hivite, generally used collectively, the Hivites (LXX. Εὐαῖος), a Canaanitish nation dwelling at the foot of Hermon and Antilibanus ( Jos 11:3 Jud. 3:3 ), but also in various other places, as for instance at Gibeon ( Gen 34:2 2Sa 24:7 1Ki 9:20 Jos 11:19).
